australia.title: Mining prohibited 'in perpetuity' from culturally significant reserve
summary: Summarize this news article:
The permanent safeguarding of a remote property in far western Queensland has come as a relief for its traditional owners and the conservationists studying its unique species.
Pilungah Reserve, just east of the Northern Territory border, has been named Queensland's third special wildlife reserve.
It means the 229,000-hectare area will have national park-level protections even though it is privately owned.
"So, any harm that would come to it would be taken very personally."
Avelina Tarrago says it is a relief to have the reserve protected from mining interests.(Supplied: Bush Heritage Australia)
Ms Tarrago is the director of the Mulligan River Aboriginal Corporation, which manages land on behalf of the Wangkamahdla people.
"We have a lot of very important cultural heritage that we were very keen to ensure [was] protected," Ms Tarrago said.
The area has previously attracted interest from mining companies.
In 2023, there were two active exploration permits for rare earth minerals,includin Summarize this news article:
g lithium and copper, and authority to prospect for petroleum.
In a statement, a spokesperson for the Department of Natural Resources and Mines said a restricted area was established in 2023 to prohibit mining activities and support the creation of the reserve.
"Based on available information, the reserve is considered to have limited mining-resource potential with no active operations or historic mining activity prior to its declaration," they said.
Ms Tarrago said the decision would help ensure rock art would not be impacted, unlike in Western Australia's Pilbara,where carvings located nearer to industry were reported to be more degraded.
"It's a relief that the petroglyphs on our country are now protected and that they will be able to be maintained in the environment that they currently are," Ms Tarrago said.
The area has previously attracted interest from mining companies searching for critical minerals, including copper and zinc.(ABC Western Qld: Victoria Pengilley)
Pilungah is th

australia.title: Adelaide skipper ruled out of Showdown after brother's funeral
summary: Summarize this news article:
Adelaide Crows Captain Jordan Dawson will not play in the Showdown match on Friday following the sudden loss of his brother.
Coach Matthew Nicks confirmed on Thursday that Dawson attended his brother's funeral at Robe on Wednesday.
"It's as you can imagine, a big week for the family and the community of Robe, yesterday was a really special day," he said.
"The entire community came together and just bonded and they were there for the family.
"Hopefully for Jordan and his family it allows them to then work on what's next."
The 59th Showdown will kick off at Adelaide Oval tomorrow night at 7.40pm (ACDT).
Jordan Dawson at a match earlier this year, before his brother's sudden death.(Getty Images: Quinn Rooney)
When asked how Dawson was doing, Nicks said he was "as well as he can be" and that the club will give "Jordan what Jordan needs".
"We don't know if he'll come to the game tomorrow night," he said.
"We'll just give him whatever time he needs.
"I'm pretty sure he'll be keen to get back Summarize this news article:
in as soon as he can."
This comes after Dawson broke down in tears and shared hugs with Brisbane players after playing on Sunday — in his firstmatch since his brother's death.
Mental health has been a hot topic in the AFL community this past month with Fremantle Dockers' player Nathan O'Driscoll most recently sharing his mental healthstruggles in a post to social media.

This came after Carlton's Elijah Hollands suffered a "mental health episode" two weeks earlierduring a match with Collingwood. over over over their over their their over over had their over one over over one one one a one one over one their one one their their one a their one

australia.title: US star on how A-League Women can 'compete with major leagues'
summary: Summarize this news article:
Taylor Otto knows a thing or two about rivalries.
The Melbourne City defender grew up in the Midwest of America and played college soccer for North Carolina, which has a renowned rivalry with Duke University.
Then, after a stint in the National Women's Soccer League, she found her way to Scottish heavyweights Celtic, whose intense rivalry with Rangers is one of the best known in the world.
It's an antagonism that extends well beyond the pitch.
For their clashes, half the stadium would have to be blocked off for Celtic fans and the other half for Rangers to keep them apart.
"They were really intense there about it. But those are the best games to be a part of," Otto said.
"It's what people look forward to, what the fans look forward to, everyone just absolutely loves it. It's what the town's talking about."
Otto is now deeply embedded at City, where she has spent the past three seasons helping the club secure premiership plates for each of those years.
She learnt very quickly who her ne Summarize this news article:
w nemesis was: Melbourne Victory.
Taylor Otto controls the ball under pressure from Rachel Lowe last year.(Getty: Graham Denholm)
"Whenever you get to a team, there's always just one team that the club just doesn't like," Otto said.
"It has nothing to do with the players. It's the club.
"You are like, 'Yep, that's a team that we never want to lose to.'"
Having played across the globe, and a keen observer of the sport,Otto sees a lot of potential in the Australian domestic league. She thinks it just needs a bigger investment to get there.
She has taken to the lifestyle of the country she now calls home, but genuinely sees the A-League Women as a competition in which world-class players could want to play.
"The … thing that I think is really challenging for leagues on the women's side is to have a competitive league from top to bottom, which you guys have," she said.
Despite the recent hype of the Matildas, support for the A-League Women remains relatively low.

india.title: US to attack Iran again? 'Short and powerful' wave of strikes may be in store
summary: Summarize this news article:

NEW DELHI: The US Central Command has prepared plans for a âshort and powerfulâ wave of strikes on Iran, likely targeting key infrastructure, according to an Axios report, as President Donald Trump is set to be briefed on fresh military options on Thursday.

The briefing, to be led by CENTCOM Commander Admiral Brad Cooper, signals that Trump is weighing the possibility of resuming major combat operations, either to break the deadlock in ongoing negotiations or to deliver a decisive blow before ending the conflict.

According to the report, CENTCOM has drawn up a plan for a âshort and powerfulâ wave of strikes on Iran, aimed at pushing Tehran back to the negotiating table with greater flexibility on the nuclear issue. Another option expected to be presented involves taking control of parts of the Strait of Hormuz to reopen it to commercial shipping, potentially involving the deployment of ground forces.
A further proposal under consideration includes a special forces operation to  Summarize this news article:
secure Iranâs stockpile of highly enriched uranium, the report said.
Trump told Axios on Wednesday that he viewed the ongoing naval blockade against Iran as âsomewhat more effective than the bombing.â
Sources cited in the report said the US president currently sees the blockade as his primary leverage but remains open to military action if Iran does not yield.
US military planners are also assessing the possibility of Iranian retaliation against American forces in the region in response to the blockade.
Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff General Dan Caine is also expected to attend the briefing, sources said. The White House did not respond to requests for comment.

The development comes against the backdrop of a similar briefing delivered by Cooper on February 26, two days before the US and Israel launched military operations against Iran. One source close to Trump told Axios that the earlier briefing played a role in the decision to go to war.

india.title: Chlorine gas leaks at defunct water purification plant in Pune; 24 hospitalised
summary: Summarize this news article:
PUNE: Twenty-four persons hospitalised after chlorine gas leakage in Pune's Kondhwa area on Thursday, according to news agency PTI.
The leak originated from an abandoned tank containing chlorine at a defunct water purification plant godown in the Gangadham area around 1 am, according to officials.

"Following the leak and the subsequent spread of gas, residents in the vicinity complained of breathlessness. Upon receiving the call, fire teams were dispatched. Several citizens were evacuated, and the leaking tank was patched using safety equipment," a fire official said.
During the operation, 22 residents and two fire personnel complained of breathlessness and were rushed to the hospital, he said.
A man who previously operated the water purification plant at the site said he had dismantled the unit, but the tank containing chlorine appeared to have remained unattended and developed a leak.
"All the affected residents and the two fire personnel are out of danger and currently under observa Summarize this news article:

tion at Sassoon General Hospital," the official said. one only only only one still over you only only their only over only one only one over only only had only only our their only only just only only a only only over had only their their their only one Only only only as only only un only only - only only " only only Only only one our only only both only only- only only every over only had had only Only one only their had only had their only had Only only un Only only had one only Only had only one had only both one only had both only had our only had - only one both only both both only Only both only one their only both had only - one only both

india.title: 'High turnout definitely due to SIR': Vote-shy Kolkata catches up with West Bengal, almost
summary: Summarize this news article:

KOLKATA: Kolkata, which traditionally registers a low turnout, voted almost on a par with the rest of Bengal on Wednesday. The EC data shows that till 9 pm, Kolkata recorded over 88.4 polling percentage, while the figure was 60.2%, the lowest in Bengal, in the 2021 Assembly elections.

Kolkata South, with four Assembly constituencies of Kolkata Port, Bhowanipore, Rashbehari and Ballygunge, recorded a polling percentage of 87.7 and Kolkata North 89.3. Bhowanipore, the most keenly watched constituency, where CM Mamata Banerjee and leader of Opposition Suvendu Adhikari contested, recorded 86.6% polling till 9 pm. In 2021, Bhowanipore, which had 2,06,319 electors, had recorded a voter turnout of 61.3%. This time, Bhowanipore's total number of electors dropped to 1,60,313, following SIR.

In 2021, Kolkata North logged 59.4% EVM polled votes and Kolkata South 61.6%. The 11 constituencies under Kolkata North and South electoral districts had 23,94,993 electors in 2021, but this time, the figure Summarize this news article:
dropped to 17,63,345, a slash of 6,31,648 electors. In 2021, 14,42, 480 electors had cast their votes in these 11 constituencies.

According to EC data, Chowringhee in Kolkata North, which faced 87,725 SIR deletions â the highest in the state â saw 86.6% voter turnout till 9 pm. Chowringhee had 2,08,172 electors in 2021 but this time, the figure came down to 1,26,349. In 2021, it had recorded 53.2% voter turnout.

Jorasanko, which saw SIR deletion of 77,993 voters, recorded 86.6% voter turnout till 9 pm, while last Assembly polls saw a 49.8% turnout. The constituency, which had 1,97,931 electors in 2021, now has 1,22,686.

In 2021, the rest five constituencies in Kolkata North districtâEntally, Beleghata, Shyampukur, Maniktala and Cossipore-Belgachhiaâhad recorded voter turnout of 67.6%, 62.7%, 57.8%, 62.6% and 59.6%. Despite significant SIR deletions, Entally, Beleghata, Shyampukur, Maniktala and Cossipore-Belgachhia this time saw 92%, 90.7%, 88%, 90.2% and 88.9% turnout till 9p

india.title: Shardul Thakur: Impact sub without impact as Mumbai Indians’ call raises questions
summary: Summarize this news article:

as an Impact Sub has triggered a wave of fan criticism after the all-rounder, who was introduced in the seventh over of the MI vs SRH fixture in IPL 2026, ended up not bowling a single delivery - despite MI bowlers enduring a torrid day with the ball at the Wankhede.
The move quickly became one of the biggest talking points from the match, with fans questioning the logic behind bringing in a frontline seam-bowling all-rounder and then not utilising him at all.

After posting 243 on the board, MI were in for a rude shock as Abhishek Sharma and Travis Head raced to 115/0 in just seven overs. MI, who made a host of changes to their playing XI, decided to sub out wicketkeeper-batter Robin Minz in favour of Thakur, hoping to break the partnership and gain an extra bowling cushion.

But Thakur ended up not getting a bowl as SRH cantered home with a record chase with over an over to spare. On a day when every MI bowler was taken to the cleaners, it was a baffling move not to hand the ball to Th Summarize this news article:
akur to stem the flow of runs from SRH batters.

Trent Boult conceded 41 runs in his four overs, while Jasprit Bumrah endured an unforgettable day, returning figures of 4-0-54-0. Allah Ghazanfar, despite picking up two wickets, also leaked runs, giving away 51 in his four overs, while Ashwani Kumar returned figures of 2-0-41-0.

Hardik Pandya went at an economy of 10.63, while Will Jacksâ solitary over cost 19 runs. Yet, there was no sign of Thakur, who has 113 wickets from 112 matches in the IPL and is generally known as a partnership-breaker.
Hereâs how social media reacted to the bizarre decision:
MI bowlers were taken apart by SRH batters, with none conceding less than 10 runs per over. Notably, the ever-reliable Jasprit Bumrah returned figures of 0/54 in four overs.

Mumbai Indians, powered by a 55-ball unbeaten 123 from Ryan Rickelton, posted 243/5 in 20 overs after electing to bat first. However, Sunrisers Hyderabad, led by Travis Head (76 off 30 balls), Heinrich Klaasen (65* o
